name: Embún - Alto de Sueña Loop
name_original: Sierra de los Dos Ríos – circular desde Embún al Alto de Sueña (R6)
description: This moderate 13.0-mile mountain biking route from Embún to Alto de Sueña offers challenging climbs and rewarding descents with great views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Valle De Hecho offers diverse terrain within the Aragonese Pyrenees, characterized by dense forests, river valleys, and prominent peaks. The region is part of the Western Valleys Natural Park, providing a protected environment for outdoor activities. Its landscape features include deep gorges like Boca del Infierno and unique river meanders in Valle de Aguas Tuertas. The area is equipped with a dedicated BTT Center, offering an extensive network of mountain bike trails suitable for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Valle De Hecho The most popular mountain bike trail is Parque Natural de los Valles Occidentales – Aguas Tuertas desde Selva de Oza (R1-Oza), a 13.2 miles (21.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 59 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park and the unique Aguas Tuertas valley. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Embún - Alto de Sueña Loop, a moderate 13.0 miles (20.9 km) path. This loop offers varied terrain and takes approximately 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. Local mountain bikers also love the Lo Serrano y Sierra de los Rios – circular from Hecho (R3+R4), a 17.0 miles (27.4 km) trail leading through the Sierra de los Rios, often completed in about 3 hours 17 minutes. Mountain biking around Valle De Hecho is defined by Pyrenean peaks, dense forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult ascents. The routes in Valle De Hecho are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 280 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Valle De Hecho's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Valle De Hecho?
A: Valle De Hecho offers a comprehensive network of mountain bike trails. Our guide features 10 curated routes, but the region's "Los Valles On Bike BTT Center" boasts over 150 kilometers of trails, including 70 km of singletrack, catering to all skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Valle De Hecho?
A: The varied scenery and extensive network of tracks in Valle De Hecho make it an ideal location for mountain biking throughout the year. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and stable weather conditions for exploring the trails.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Valle De Hecho is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975743459/parque-natural-de-los-valles-occidentales-aguas-tuertas-desde-selva-de-oza-r1-oza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Aguas Tuertas&lt;/a&gt; with its unique river meanders, or ride through the dense forests of Selva de Oza. The dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo d'Acher&lt;/a&gt; is also a prominent feature in the landscape.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Valle De Hecho offers trails for various skill levels. While our guide includes 5 moderate and 5 difficult routes, the BTT Center ensures options for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975743459/parque-natural-de-los-valles-occidentales-aguas-tuertas-desde-selva-de-oza-r1-oza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de los Valles Occidentales – Aguas Tuertas desde Selva de Oza (R1-Oza)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that explores the natural park and is a good option for those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Valle De Hecho?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Valle De Hecho are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975835069/embun-alto-de-suena-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embún - Alto de Sueña Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975806128/lo-serrano-y-sierra-de-los-rios-circular-from-hecho-r3+r4" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lo Serrano y Sierra de los Rios – circular from Hecho (R3+R4)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in this region?
A: The mountain bike trails in Valle De Hecho range from moderate to difficult. Our selection includes 5 moderate routes, which are suitable for riders with some experience, and 5 difficult routes, offering more challenging ascents and technical sections for advanced riders.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sights accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the region blends natural beauty with cultural heritage. You can explore charming traditional villages like Hecho and Siresa. The Selva de Oza also features an ancient Roman road, offering a glimpse into the area's past while you ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Valle De Hecho?
A: The mountain bike trails in Valle De Hecho are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning Pyrenean landscapes, and the extensive network of trails that cater to different abilities.

Q: Is it possible to see wildlife while mountain biking in Valle De Hecho?
A: Valle De Hecho is part of the Western Valleys Natural Park and is known for its rich biodiversity. It's home to diverse wildlife, including chamois and marmots, and over 200 bird species. Keep an eye out, especially in quieter areas, for opportunities to spot local fauna.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural swimming spots near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the Aragón-Subordán river and its tributaries flow through the valley, creating refreshing natural swimming holes and clear waterfalls. The impressive Boca del Infierno gorge, carved by the river, is a spectacular natural formation and a popular spot for canyoning, offering tranquil yet striking scenery.

Q: Are there any mountain peaks I can see or ride towards in Valle De Hecho?
A: The valley is encircled by numerous impressive peaks, ranging from 2,300 to 2,700 meters. Notable peaks include Peña Forca, Punta Agüerri, and the visually striking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo d'Acher&lt;/a&gt;, providing dramatic backdrops and panoramic views for your rides.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail featured in the guide?
A: The longest mountain bike trail in our guide is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20520443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Achar of Aguas Tuertas – Aguas Tuertas Valley loop from Borda de Catarecha&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a distance of approximately 35 kilometers (21.7 miles) and is rated as difficult.
tips: View of the Ansó valley from Punta de Sueña, one of the vertices of the Sierra de los Dos Ríos that separates the Echo and Ansó valleys.

Crossed by the Aragón Subordán, it sits on a hill on the right bank of the river that is crossed by a curious bridge with three arches. The parish church of San Martin is interesting and has an altarpiece of San Francisco de Asis with two female busts known as the devils of Embún.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Sierra de los Dos Ríos – circular desde Embún al Alto de Sueña (R6) mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is officially classified as moderate. While it's not technically difficult, it demands a good level of physical fitness due to its sustained and steep uphill gradients, especially on the ascent to Alto de Sueña.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll start on the Ribera track from Embún, then encounter challenging "Z"-shaped switchbacks during the climb to Alto de Sueña. After the summit, the trail continues on an undulating track towards Collada Arto, followed by a long descent back to Embún. The route offers a mix of Pyrenean and Pre-Pyrenean landscapes.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the ascent to Alto de Sueña itself provides a significant physical challenge and likely offers expansive views from its 1,248-meter summit. You'll also pass by View of the Ansó Valley, which is a great spot to take in the scenery. The charming Embún Village, where the route starts and ends, is also a highlight.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sierra de los Dos Ríos R6 trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Embún Village. You should be able to find suitable parking within or near the village to begin your ride. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked for visitors.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de los Dos Ríos R6 mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, mountain biking trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where you might encounter livestock or other trail users. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and elevation gain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Embún area, with its blend of mountain and pre-mountain scenery, is ideal for mountain biking throughout the year. However, for the most comfortable experience, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring colder conditions or snow at higher elevations like Alto de Sueña.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: No, typically you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to ride the Sierra de los Dos Ríos R6 mountain bike trail. It's open for public access.

faqs.question: Are there any quicker descent options on this circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, for those looking for a faster return, there is a rapid and easy descent option from Collado de la Carroza that rejoins the Ribera track, providing a straightforward downhill section back towards Embún.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through small sections of the Viejo Camino de Santiago por el Valle de Hecho - Calzada del Summo Pyreneo and the Camino de Santiago GR65.3.3.

faqs.question: What amenities are available in Embún Village?
faqs.answer: Embún is a charming village and, as a starting point for outdoor activities, it typically offers basic amenities. You might find local cafes, pubs, or small accommodations. It's a good idea to check in advance for specific services if you plan to stay or eat there.
